description:
You are assisting a researcher who is standardising the format of a small set of laboratory readings.

    Current situation
    1. A raw, space-separated text file already exists at  
       /home/user/datasets/measurements_2023.txt
    2. The file contains **exactly** five lines, each with three
       space-delimited fields in this order:  
       • experiment identifier (string, no spaces)  
       • measured value (floating-point number)  
       • ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD)

    Objective
    1. Convert the raw file into a clean CSV file named  
       /home/user/datasets/measurements_2023.csv
    2. Add a header row to the CSV that reads (all lowercase, commas exactly as shown)  
       experiment_id,value,date
    3. Preserve the original row order when copying the data into the CSV, replacing the spaces with single commas.
    4. Produce a small summary report called  
       /home/user/datasets/measurements_summary.txt  
       The summary report must contain **exactly two lines**:
       Line 1: “Total rows: 5”  
       Line 2: “Average value: 22.0”  
       (Average is the arithmetic mean of the second column, printed with **one** digit after the decimal point.)
    5. Do not delete or modify the original measurements_2023.txt file.

    Expected file contents after completion

    a) /home/user/datasets/measurements_2023.csv
       ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │ experiment_id,value,date                     │
       │ EXP001,23.4,2023-05-01                       │
       │ EXP002,19.8,2023-05-02                       │
       │ EXP003,25.1,2023-05-03                       │
       │ EXP004,21.0,2023-05-04                       │
       │ EXP005,20.7,2023-05-05                       │
       └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    b) /home/user/datasets/measurements_summary.txt
       ┌──────────────────────────┐
       │ Total rows: 5            │
       │ Average value: 22.0      │
       └──────────────────────────┘

    Hints
    • A combination of “awk” or “sed” can be used to replace whitespace with commas and
      insert the header in a single command.
    • “awk” can also be used to calculate the average.
    • You should only need 2–3 well-composed shell commands in total.
truth:
Initial file already present before the agent starts:

    Path: /home/user/datasets/measurements_2023.txt  
    Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)  
    Contents (each line ends with LF only):
    EXP001 23.4 2023-05-01
    EXP002 19.8 2023-05-02
    EXP003 25.1 2023-05-03
    EXP004 21.0 2023-05-04
    EXP005 20.7 2023-05-05

    Files that MUST exist after correct completion:

    1. /home/user/datasets/measurements_2023.csv  
       Permissions: 644  
       Exact byte-for-byte content including trailing newline:

       experiment_id,value,date\n
       EXP001,23.4,2023-05-01\n
       EXP002,19.8,2023-05-02\n
       EXP003,25.1,2023-05-03\n
       EXP004,21.0,2023-05-04\n
       EXP005,20.7,2023-05-05\n

    2. /home/user/datasets/measurements_summary.txt  
       Permissions: 644  
       Exact content including trailing newline:

       Total rows: 5\n
       Average value: 22.0\n
